name: Crispy Fried Whole Fish with Bacon-Potato Salad
recipeInstructions:
- 5 slices bacon, julienned
- 1/4 cup balsamic vinegar
- 2 tablespoons chopped scallions
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- Salt and black pepper
- 2 cups flour
- 2 tablespoons Creole spice
- 2 eggs
- 2 small trout
- Chopped parsley, for garnish
1. Boil potatoes until tender; drain.
2. Cut potatoes into halves if small, or quarters if larger.
3. Meanwhile, cook bacon in a large skillet until crisp. Using a slotted spoon, scoop up bacon pieces and add to potatoes with vinegar, scallions, and 1 tablespoon of oil. Season and set aside, keeping salad warm.
4. In the same skillet, heat remaining oil until bubbly. Combine flour and 1 tablespoon Creole spice in a shallow bowl.
5. In another shallow bowl, whisk eggs with a pinch of seasoning.
6. Season fish with remaining Creole spice. Dredge fish in seasoned flour, shaking off excess; dip in egg wash; dredge again in flour.
7. Place fish immediately in the sizzling skillet. Cook until golden and crisp, about 8 minutes total (flip halfway through).
8. Spoon potato salad onto 2 dinner plates and top with fish. Garnish with chopped parsley.
Yield: 2 servings
